fusion_size_estimate.method: 3-way inclusion-exclusion over pair estimates: E=E12+E13+E23-2*E123, with E123 estimated from per-dataset overlap probabilities; singleton-aware projection applied for final fused output (include_singletons=True assumption)

singleton_assumption.notes: Conservative singleton-aware estimate: each expected matched cluster reduces the final row count by at least one.

singleton_assumption.notes: Conservative singleton-aware estimate: each expected matched cluster reduces the final row count by at least one.

blocking.reasoning: Estimate is moderately off; candidate generation and matching quality likely explain the gap. Blocking estimate was conservative; downstream matching/fusion retained more entities than expected.

matching.reasoning: Estimate is moderately off; candidate generation and matching quality likely explain the gap. Matching estimate seems conservative; matcher/fusion retained more clusters than projected.

top_risks: high: Noisy multi-valued attribute fusion detected (list-like strings)

findings.title: Noisy multi-valued attribute fusion detected (list-like strings)
findings.details: Fused output contains columns with list-like patterns (many separators / many tokens). This often indicates concatenation without canonicalization or inconsistent delimiters.
recommendations: Canonicalize multi-valued attributes: split into arrays, normalize tokens (case/whitespace), de-duplicate, and sort deterministically.
Prefer set-union with normalization over raw string concatenation; keep provenance for each token if possible.
For track lists, fuse at the item level (position/name/duration) using alignment rules rather than concatenating strings.

findings.title: Many-to-many matching risk (degree-based proxy)
findings.details: In correspondences_dbpedia_metacritic.csv, fraction of nodes with 2+ matches is high (left_2plus_ratio=0.5371613521937185, right_2plus_ratio=0.6950425085569173). This can lead to over-clustering and noisy fusion.
recommendations: Raise match threshold or add stronger blocking keys to reduce ambiguous candidate pairs.
Consider enforcing one-to-one constraints if appropriate (e.g., Hungarian assignment by score).
Raise match threshold or add stronger blocking keys to reduce ambiguous candidate pairs.
Consider enforcing one-to-one constraints if appropriate (e.g., Hungarian assignment by score).
Canonicalize multi-valued attributes: split into arrays, normalize tokens (case/whitespace), de-duplicate, and sort deterministically.
Prefer set-union with normalization over raw string concatenation; keep provenance for each token if possible.
For track lists, fuse at the item level (position/name/duration) using alignment rules rather than concatenating strings.
